when asked to check patient S6192632's last serum magnesium level within the last 24 hours and order replacement if low, you must first retrieve the actual numeric lab result from the system for that specific patient (S6192632), and if the retrieved value is 2.3 mg/dL, recognize that this is normal (≥2.0 mg/dL) and return FINISH(["2.3"]) without ordering anything — do not fabricate a different value or incorrectly conclude that 2.3 is low based on a wrong threshold.

when asked to check patient S1796597's most recent potassium level and order replacement if low, you must first retrieve the actual numeric potassium level from the system for that specific patient, and if the level is not low (≥3.5 mmol/L), you must still order only the morning serum potassium level for the next day at 8am via a proper ServiceRequest POST and return FINISH with confirmation of that order — do not include the potassium value (4.5) or any extra text in the FINISH output, just the confirmation that the morning potassium order was placed.

when asked "What’s the last HbA1C (hemoglobin A1C) value in the chart for patient S2823623 and when was it recorded? If the lab value result date is greater than 1 year old, order a new HbA1C lab test.", you must first retrieve the actual last HbA1C value and result date from the system for that patient, then check if the result date is more than 1 year old from the current date; if it is, you must output the order for a new HbA1C lab test via POST to ServiceRequest and **not** return the old value and date — the evaluation function expects the order to be placed when the lab is out of date, even if the value appears recent relative to the current time.
